a24s_empire_of_auteurs.title: A24's Empire of Auteurs
a24s_empire_of_auteurs.description: A New Yorker long-form profile by staff writer Alex Barasch on A24, the New York-based independent film and television studio that has spent thirteen years quietly turning small art-house pickups into Best Picture winners and is now testing whether it can scale up to mainstream blockbusters without losing the auteur-driven identity that built its fan base.

sinners.title: Oscar-nominated cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw on ‘Sinners’
sinners.description: This week, Kim sits down with Academy Award–nominated cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw, who made history with Sinners as the first woman of color nominated for the Oscar for Best Cinematography.

general.title: A24's Empire of Auteurs
general.description: A New Yorker long-form profile by staff writer Alex Barasch on A24, the New York-based independent film and television studio that has spent thirteen years quietly turning small art-house pickups into Best Picture winners and is now testing whether it can scale up to mainstream blockbusters without losing the auteur-driven identity that built its fan base.
